title: FetchNodeConfig
description: Config for core.openwop.http.fetch. The URL template and method are configured here; per-request body / dynamic substitutions arrive via inputs.

url.description: Request URL. MAY contain `{{name}}` template placeholders replaced from `inputs.variables` at dispatch. The substituted URL MUST resolve to an `http://` or `https://` scheme; other schemes are rejected at runtime with `INVALID_URL`.

method.description: HTTP method. Defaults to GET. Methods that conventionally carry a body (POST/PUT/PATCH) consume `inputs.body`; others ignore it.

headers.description: Static request headers. The `Authorization` header, if present, is sent verbatim — workflow authors needing dynamic Bearer auth typically resolve it through the host's secret-injection mechanism and pass the resolved value here at dispatch.

timeoutMs.description: Per-attempt request timeout in milliseconds. Bounded to 2 minutes so a hung remote can't stall a run indefinitely.

retry.description: Retry policy. Applies to 5xx responses and network errors only — 4xx responses are NEVER retried (they're treated as terminal client errors).

maxAttempts.description: Maximum total attempts including the first try. `maxAttempts: 1` disables retries.

initialDelayMs.description: Delay before the second attempt. Doubles each subsequent attempt (exponential).

idempotency.description: Idempotency-Key handling. By default the node derives a deterministic key from (runId, nodeId, body-hash) so replays generate the same key — letting the remote server dedupe. Set `mode: 'disabled'` to suppress the header entirely (workflows for endpoints that error on unknown headers).

mode.description: `auto` (default) sends `Idempotency-Key: <derived>` on every non-GET non-HEAD non-OPTIONS request. `disabled` omits the header.
